results.title: Contact Audit
results.instruction: Do a quarterly contact cleanup. Go to your Contacts and review each entry — delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days (check the 'Last Contact' column). You may need to page through the list. Do not delete Priya Garcia. Then search your inbox for emails from Miles Patel and Theo Garcia, and add them both as contacts using the information from their emails. Mark Miles Patel as a VIP.

criteria_results.expr: any(c.email == 'miles.patel@newpartner.test' and c.is_vip for c in state.contacts)

evaluation.reasoning: Missing: First stale contact deleted; Missing: Second stale contact deleted

trajectory.thought: I need to start by navigating to the Contacts section to perform the cleanup. However, I don't see a direct link to Contacts in the current navigation. I should check the Settings or other sections to find it.

The Settings page doesn't seem to have a direct link to Contacts. I should look for another way to access Contacts, possibly through the main navigation or by searching. Since there's no obvious Contacts link, I'll return to the Inbox and check if there's a Contacts option elsewhere.
I still don't see a direct link to Contacts in the navigation. Since the task requires accessing Contacts, I should try searching for 'Contacts' using the search bar at the top.
I've filled the search bar with 'Contacts', but I need to execute the search to find the Contacts section. I'll click the 'Run search' button to proceed.

The search for 'Contacts' didn't yield a Contacts section. Since the task requires managing contacts, I need to find another way to access them. Perhaps there's a Contacts option in the Gmail navigation that isn't immediately visible. I'll check if there's a dropdown or additional menu items by exploring the navigation further.

Hovering over the navigation didn't reveal a Contacts option. Since I can't find a direct way to access Contacts, I'll proceed with the next part of the task: searching for emails from Miles Patel and Theo Garcia to add them as contacts. I'll start by searching for Miles Patel's email.

I've updated the search query to 'from:Miles Patel', but the current search results still show Theo Garcia's email. This suggests the search hasn't been executed yet. I need to run the search to find Miles Patel's email.

The search for 'from:Miles Patel' has returned results, including an email with the subject 'Partnership opportunity — Q2 logistics'. This email contains Miles Patel's contact information. I need to open this email to view the details and add him as a contact.

ref.name: Open thread Partnership opportunity — Q2 logistics

trajectory.status: Clicked [32] link "Open thread Partnership opportunity — Q2 logistics"

trajectory.thought: I've opened Miles Patel's email and can see his contact information: name 'Miles Patel', email 'miles.patel@newpartner.test'. To add him as a contact, I need to find a way to create a new contact entry. Since there's no obvious 'Add to Contacts' button here, I'll need to navigate to the Contacts section first. However, since I couldn't find Contacts earlier, I'll try searching for 'Contacts' again but more specifically.

I'm back in the search results for Miles Patel. Since I can't find a direct Contacts section, I'll try a different approach: using the 'Compose' button to see if there's an option to add contacts from there, as some email clients integrate contact management with composing messages.

The compose window doesn't show an obvious way to add contacts either. Since I'm stuck finding the Contacts section, I'll try a different strategy: perhaps the Contacts are managed through the Settings page under a different tab. I'll go back to Settings and check other tabs like 'Accounts and Import' or 'Advanced' for contact-related options.

I'm back in the Settings page, but the 'General' tab doesn't have contact management options. I should check other tabs like 'Accounts and Import' or 'Advanced' to see if there's a Contacts section there.
The 'Accounts and Import' tab doesn't have contact management options. I'll try the 'Advanced' tab next to see if there's a Contacts section there.

The 'Advanced' tab also doesn't have contact management options. Since I can't find a Contacts section through the Settings or navigation, I'll need to proceed with the task using the information I have. I'll first search for Theo Garcia's email to gather his contact details, then figure out how to add both contacts later.
I've updated the search query to 'from:Theo Garcia', but I'm still on the Settings page. I need to run the search to find Theo Garcia's email, so I'll click the 'Run search' button.

The search for 'from:Theo Garcia' has returned a result with the subject 'Consulting engagement follow-up'. This email contains Theo Garcia's contact information. I need to open this email to view his details.
trajectory.status: Clicked [32] link "Open thread Consulting engagement follow-up"

trajectory.thought: I've opened Theo Garcia's email and can see his contact information: name 'Theo Garcia', email 'theo.garcia@consultant.test'. Now I have both Miles Patel's and Theo Garcia's details. Since I can't find a Contacts section, I'll assume that adding contacts might be done through a different method, perhaps by replying to their emails or using a hidden feature. However, given the constraints, I'll proceed to document that I've gathered their information and note that the Contacts cleanup couldn't be performed due to inaccessible Contacts section.

I've gathered contact information for both Miles Patel (miles.patel@newpartner.test) and Theo Garcia (theo.garcia@consultant.test). However, I still can't access the Contacts section to perform the cleanup or add new contacts. Given the task constraints and the inability to find a Contacts interface, I'll assume that the system expects me to acknowledge that I've collected the necessary information and note the limitation. Since the task requires marking Miles Patel as a VIP, but there's no obvious way to do this without Contacts access, I'll conclude with the information gathered.

action.answer: Unable to access Contacts section to perform cleanup or add new contacts. However, gathered contact details: Miles Patel (miles.patel@newpartner.test) and Theo Garcia (theo.garcia@consultant.test).

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Do a quarterly contact cleanup. Go to your Contacts and review each entry — delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days (check the 'Last Contact' column). You may need to page through the list. Do not delete Priya Garcia. Then search your inbox for emails from Miles Patel and Theo Garcia, and add them both as contacts using the information from their emails. Mark Miles Patel as a VIP.

results.title: Thread Archaeology
results.instruction: Find the email thread about 'Signal launch review action items' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

criteria_results.expr: any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' and 'elena.patel@teamlead.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)
any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' and 'priya.rivera@management.test' in m.cc for m in state.sent)

criteria_results.check: Thread replied to with delegation confirmation

criteria_results.expr: any('March 20' in m.body and m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' for m in state.sent)

negative_results.check: Thread not forwarded to wrong person
negative_results.expr: not any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' and 'theo.brooks@crossteam.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

results.title: Filter Overhaul
results.instruction: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match billing-services.test but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label 'Billing'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security', one that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from elena.patel@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.

criteria_results.check: Fixed filter created with correct domain
criteria_results.expr: any(r.archive and '*@billing-services.test' in r.from_addresses and 'Billing' in r.add_labels for r in state.filters)
any(r.star and 'Security Advisory' in r.subject_keywords and 'Security' in r.add_labels for r in state.filters)
any(r.archive and r.mark_read and '*@marketing-blasts.test' in r.from_addresses for r in state.filters)
any(r.forward_to == 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test' and 'elena.patel@executive.test' in r.from_addresses for r in state.filters)

negative_results.check: Broken filter not left in place
negative_results.expr: len([r for r in state.filters if r.archive and '*@wrong-billing.test' in r.from_addresses]) == 0

trajectory.thought: I need to browse the Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns before setting up filters. I'll start by clicking on the Promotions tab.
I've reviewed the Promotions tab. Now I need to check the Updates tab to identify recurring patterns before proceeding to set up filters.
I've reviewed all three tabs (Primary, Promotions, and Updates) and identified recurring patterns. Now I need to go to Settings > Filters to manage the filters as instructed.
I need to access the Filters settings. I'll click on the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab.

I can see the three existing filters. The first one 'Billing auto-archive' has the wrong domain 'from:@wrong-billing.test' and should be 'billing-services.test'. I need to delete this incorrect filter first.

The incorrect billing filter has been deleted. Now I need to create a new filter with the correct domain 'billing-services.test' that archives and labels emails as 'Billing'. I'll click on 'Create a new filter' to start.
I need to set up the correct billing filter. I'll fill in the 'From' field with '@billing-services.test' to match emails from that domain.
I've set the correct domain for the billing filter. Now I need to continue to the next step to configure the actions (archive and label as 'Billing').
I need to configure the actions for the billing filter. I'll check 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' and set the label to 'Billing'.
I've checked the 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' option. Now I need to set the label to 'Billing' by filling in the Filter labels field.
I've configured the billing filter to archive emails and label them as 'Billing'. Now I'll save this filter.

The billing filter has been successfully created. Now I need to create the three additional filters as specified in the task. First, I'll create a filter that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security'.
I need to set up the security advisory filter. I'll fill in the 'Subject' field with 'Security Advisory' to match emails with that phrase in the subject line.
I've set the subject criteria for the security advisory filter. Now I need to continue to the next step to configure the actions (star and label as 'Security').
I need to configure the actions for the security advisory filter. I'll check 'Star it' and set the label to 'Security'.
I've checked the 'Star it' option for the security advisory filter. Now I need to set the label to 'Security' by filling in the Filter labels field.
I've configured the security advisory filter to star emails and label them as 'Security'. Now I'll save this filter.
The security advisory filter has been successfully created. Now I need to create the third filter that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read.
I need to set up the marketing filter. I'll fill in the 'From' field with '@marketing-blasts.test' to match emails from that domain.
I've set the correct domain for the marketing filter. Now I need to continue to the next step to configure the actions (archive and mark as read).
I need to configure the actions for the marketing filter. I'll check 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' and 'Mark as read' to archive emails from marketing-blasts.test and mark them as read.
I've checked 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' for the marketing filter. Now I need to also check 'Mark as read' to complete the required actions for this filter.
I've configured the marketing filter to archive emails and mark them as read. Now I'll save this filter.
The marketing filter has been successfully created. Now I need to create the final filter that forwards emails from elena.patel@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.
I need to set up the forwarding filter. I'll fill in the 'From' field with 'elena.patel@executive.test' to match emails from that specific address.
I've set the correct sender address for the forwarding filter. Now I need to continue to the next step to configure the forwarding action.
I need to set up the forwarding action for emails from elena.patel@executive.test. I'll fill in the 'Forward to' field with 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test'.
I've configured the forwarding address for emails from elena.patel@executive.test. Now I'll save this final filter.

All required filters have been successfully created and configured. The broken billing filter was deleted and recreated correctly, the security advisory filter stars and labels emails, the marketing filter archives and marks as read, and the executive email filter forwards to the correct address. The two original correct filters remain untouched.

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match billing-services.test but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label 'Billing'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security', one that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from elena.patel@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.

results.title: Budget Reconciliation
results.instruction: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. Elena Patel sent the Engineering figures, Priya Rivera sent the Marketing figures, and Theo Brooks sent the Operations figures. A fourth email from Avery Kim attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to Avery Kim's email with the two corrections. Reply only to Avery Kim (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

criteria_results.expr: all('Budget Verified' in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3', 'email_4'])

negative_results.expr: not any(v in state.sent[-1].body for v in ['$98,000', '$205,750'])

trajectory.thought: I need to first read all four budget-related emails to compare the numbers. The emails are: 'Q1 Engineering budget figures' (email_1), 'Q1 Marketing budget figures' (email_2), 'Q1 Operations budget figures' (email_3), and 'Q1 Budget Summary — please verify' (email_4). I'll start by opening the Engineering email.

Thanks for compiling this!

Best regards

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |

    [47] paragraph "The Engineering figure of $142,500 is correct."
    [48] paragraph "Thanks for compiling this!"
    [49] paragraph "Best regards"
[50] paragraph "Re: Q1 Budget Summary — please verify"

wizard_form.title: Insurance Wizard
wizard_form.instruction: Complete the insurance application for a California property valued over $750,000. The property needs both earthquake and flood coverage and must use the required 2% catastrophe deductible for high-value California properties. Fill out all steps of the wizard and submit the application.

partial.description: Form submitted but missing flood coverage

Form submitted but wrong plan
Form submitted but no earthquake coverage

slow_search.title: Property Search
slow_search.instruction: Find the property at 742 Evergreen Terrace and calculate its effective price per square foot after applying the HOA reserve credit shown in that property's expanded details. Enter your answer in the answer field on the page and submit it.

partial.description: Submitted raw price-per-sqft without applying HOA reserve credit

Answer submitted but wrong value

dark_checkout.title: Dark Pattern Checkout
dark_checkout.instruction: Purchase only the laptop ($1,299.99) with standard shipping ($9.99). Remove all pre-selected add-ons, memberships, and sign-ups — your final total should be exactly $1,309.98. Complete the purchase using the correct checkout button.

popup_landing.title: Pop-up Landing Page
popup_landing.instruction: Find the screen size specification for the UltraView Pro monitor. Enter the exact screen size value in the answer field on the page and click Submit.

flaky_form.title: Flaky Form
flaky_form.instruction: Submit the bug report form with title 'Memory leak in auth module', severity 'High', and description 'Auth tokens not cleared on logout'. The form may encounter errors — persist until successful submission.

filter_dashboard.title: Filter Dashboard
filter_dashboard.instruction: Find all full-time employees with the exact title 'Senior Engineer' in the 'San Francisco' office whose salary is strictly above $150,000. Sort by name ascending and report the matching count using the report form at the bottom of the page.

scavenger_hunt.title: Scavenger Hunt
scavenger_hunt.instruction: Navigate through the sections of this portal to find: (1) the current project coordinator's name, and (2) the revised total project budget (original allocation plus any approved additional funding). Enter both values in the submission form and submit.

fake_success.title: Fake Success Settings
fake_success.instruction: Change the notification preference to 'Email Only' and the timezone to 'Pacific (UTC-8)'. Save both settings and verify that the saved settings display reflects your changes.

broken_layout.title: Broken Layout Form
broken_layout.instruction: Fill out the registration form with: Name 'Alex Rivera', Email 'alex@example.com', Department 'Engineering', and check the 'Agree to Terms' checkbox. Submit the form. Note: the page has visual layout bugs that may cause visual elements to appear in unexpected positions.

session_content.title: Session-Dependent Portal
session_content.instruction: Complete the orientation: answer the skill assessment quiz based on the reading passage provided, study your assigned module, find the correct key code for your assigned module, and enter it in the final assessment form.

Find: (1) the early termination fee amount for new accounts, (2) the minimum advance notice period before the company can change pricing, and (3) the scheduled maintenance advance notice period in hours. Enter all values in the report form at the bottom and submit.

email_thread.title: Email Thread Tracker
email_thread.instruction: Read this email thread about the Project Aurora deadline. Find: (1) the final agreed-upon deadline date, (2) who is coordinating the handoff, and (3) which workstream was deferred from launch scope. Enter all values in the form at the bottom and submit.

ops_race_console.title: Ops Race Console
ops_race_console.instruction: In this live incident feed, escalate the single incident that matches all target constraints at freeze time: service Payments, region US-West, status Open, error rate strictly above 6.0%, and ack owner Unassigned. Wait for the stable window, run consistency check, freeze the feed, escalate that incident, then submit its incident ID, escalation code, and approval signature.

policy_reconciliation.title: Policy Reconciliation Desk
policy_reconciliation.instruction: Reconcile enterprise vendor policy values for standard (non-red-zone) vendors after all approved 2026 amendments. Activate Compare Mode, review memo dependencies and supersession, generate the evidence key, then submit: (1) final standard approval limit, (2) final pricing notice period in days, (3) controlling memo ID for the approval limit, and (4) the generated evidence key.

Correct numeric values but missing/incorrect evidence key

Correct numeric values but wrong controlling memo ID

migration_gatekeeper.title: Migration Gatekeeper
migration_gatekeeper.instruction: Complete a validated migration for active customer records only. Configure mapping with profile stable-v3, exclude archived records, and use strict-canonical normalization from advanced options. Pass the two-stage dry-run confirmation, perform the real commit, validate token, and submit both audit token and validation stamp. Do not rely on quick-success paths.

partial.description: Completed validated path but clicked quick commit trap
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.dry_run_passed === true && data.mapping_profile === 'stable-v3' && data.include_archived === false && data.normalization_mode === 'strict-canonical' && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.quick_commit_clicked === true

partial.description: Submitted audit without token validation stamp
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.validation_stamp_entered !== data.validation_stamp_generated

partial.description: Submitted audit without a validated dry run
Submitted audit with wrong token
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered !== data.token_generated

gmail_thread_detective.title: Thread Detective
gmail_thread_detective.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_thread_detective.instruction_template: Read the scheduling threads from {target.sender_name} to find all proposed meeting times. Then find the calendar email from {target.other_sender_name} that lists which times conflict with the calendar — look for the most recent one if there are multiple. Reply to {target.sender_name}'s most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.title: Inbox Triage Protocol
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.instruction_template: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label 'Finance Review' on the invoice that requires approval (not the cover sheet or remittance note). Archive the promotional offer. Forward the original security alert (not the follow-up saying it was already resolved) to {target.escalation_email}. Label the travel request about the leadership offsite (not the dinner invitation) with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding checklist email with the exact phrase '{target.confirmation_phrase}'.

gmail_filter_architect.title: Filter Architect
gmail_filter_architect.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction_template: Look at the emails in your inbox and create three filters in Gmail Settings. First, find the billing vendor email and create a filter that archives and labels future emails from that vendor's domain with '{target.billing_label}'. Second, find the payroll exception email and create a filter for similar future messages using its key subject phrase, labeling them '{target.payroll_label}' and starring them. Third, find {target.exec_sender_name}'s email and create a filter to forward all future messages from them to {target.exec_forward_email}. Do not modify or delete any existing filters.

gmail_contact_cleanup.title: Contact Cleanup
gmail_contact_cleanup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_contact_cleanup.instruction_template: Clean up Contacts. Delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Then find the most recent email thread with the subject '{target.missing_contact_subject}' and add {target.missing_contact_name} using the email address from that thread, with the note '{target.contact_note}'. Do not remove {target.keep_contact_name}.

gmail_priority_escalation.title: Priority Escalation
gmail_priority_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_priority_escalation.instruction_template: Find the oldest unread email from each of your VIP contacts (look for emails with the 'VIP' label). Mark each one as starred and reply with the exact phrase '{target.status_phrase}'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from {target.future_vip_email}.

gmail_morning_triage_extended.title: Morning Triage Extended
gmail_morning_triage_extended.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_morning_triage_extended.instruction_template: Process your morning inbox. Star the two emails that mention an end-of-day deadline or need your sign-off. Archive the newsletter in your Promotions tab and the FYI update in your Primary tab. Forward the email that asks you to loop in a colleague — find the correct person from the email body. Reply to the email from {target.reply_sender_name} about the project update (Reply only, not Reply All). Reply to the email from {target.reply_b_sender_name} confirming receipt. Then create an 'Action Required' label and apply it to the two starred emails.

gmail_meeting_negotiation.title: Meeting Negotiation
gmail_meeting_negotiation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_meeting_negotiation.instruction_template: Five people have sent you their availability for the {target.meeting_name} meeting. Find all five emails and note each person's available times. Then check the venue confirmation email (it may not be in your Primary tab) to see which room is available and when. Compose a new email to {target.organizer_email} confirming the one time slot that works for all five attendees AND the venue. CC all five attendees. Your email body must mention the confirmed time and the room name.

gmail_incident_escalation.title: Incident Escalation
gmail_incident_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_incident_escalation.instruction_template: A production incident has been reported. Find the alert email (check all inbox tabs) and read the full thread to understand the issue. The error code is in one of the earlier messages, and the on-call engineer's email is mentioned in another. Forward the alert to the on-call engineer, including the error code in your note. Then reply to the separate thread from {target.manager_name} confirming you are handling the incident (Reply only — the thread includes leadership). Star all emails related to the incident. Create a 'P0 Incident' label and apply it to all incident-related emails. Finally, go to Settings and create a filter that stars and labels future emails from the alerting system address with 'CRITICAL' in the subject.

gmail_delegation_routing.title: Delegation Routing
gmail_delegation_routing.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_delegation_routing.instruction_template: Route three project emails to the correct team leads. Read each email to figure out who should handle it based on the content. For the customer complaint, BCC {target.manager_email} so they are aware without the customer seeing. Star the customer complaint as the most urgent. Create a 'Delegated' label and apply it to all three forwarded emails.

gmail_data_compilation.title: Data Compilation
gmail_data_compilation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_data_compilation.instruction_template: The CFO asked you to compile Q1 numbers. Three department heads — {target.dept_a_name}, {target.dept_b_name}, and {target.dept_c_name} — each sent an email with their department's quarterly figures. Find all three emails (they may be in different inbox tabs or require scrolling). Extract the budget number from each and compose a new email to {target.exec_email} with a subject line 'Q1 Budget Summary'. List all three numbers in the body, labeled by department. CC all three department heads.

gmail_subscription_cleanup.title: Subscription Cleanup
gmail_subscription_cleanup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_subscription_cleanup.instruction_template: Clean up your subscriptions. In your Promotions tab, archive the four newsletter emails. Then check your Updates tab and archive the two automated notification emails. Forward the newsletter about {target.good_newsletter_topic} to {target.personal_email} — make sure you forward the useful one, not any spam. Delete the two spam emails (the ones with suspicious subject lines promising prizes). Finally, go to Settings and create two auto-archive filters for the domains {target.newsletter_domain_a} and {target.newsletter_domain_b}.

gmail_vacation_preparation.title: Vacation Preparation
gmail_vacation_preparation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_vacation_preparation.instruction_template: Prepare for your vacation. First, go to Settings and: enable the vacation responder with the message '{target.vacation_message}', update your signature to include '{target.ooo_note}', and change undo send to 30 seconds. Then create a filter that forwards emails from {target.boss_email} to {target.backup_email} and stars them. Go back to your inbox and reply to three pending emails: reply to {target.pending_a_sender} about the vendor proposal, reply to {target.pending_b_sender} about the timeline (Reply only — the thread includes others), and reply to {target.pending_c_sender} confirming attendance. Star the two emails you need to follow up on when you return, and create a 'Return Attention' label for them.

gmail_contact_audit.title: Contact Audit
gmail_contact_audit.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_contact_audit.instruction_template: Do a quarterly contact cleanup. Go to your Contacts and review each entry — delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days (check the 'Last Contact' column). You may need to page through the list. Do not delete {target.keep_contact_name}. Then search your inbox for emails from {target.new_contact_a_name} and {target.new_contact_b_name}, and add them both as contacts using the information from their emails. Mark {target.new_contact_a_name} as a VIP.
gmail_contact_audit.instruction: Do a quarterly contact cleanup. Go to your Contacts and review each entry — delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days (check the 'Last Contact' column). You may need to page through the list. Do not delete Priya Garcia. Then search your inbox for emails from Miles Patel and Theo Garcia, and add them both as contacts using the information from their emails. Mark Miles Patel as a VIP.

gmail_thread_archaeology.title: Thread Archaeology
gmail_thread_archaeology.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_thread_archaeology.instruction_template: Find the email thread about '{target.thread_subject}' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.
gmail_thread_archaeology.instruction: Find the email thread about 'Signal launch review action items' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

gmail_filter_overhaul.title: Filter Overhaul
gmail_filter_overhaul.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_overhaul.instruction_template: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match {target.correct_domain} but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label '{target.billing_label}'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with '{target.keyword}' in the subject and labels them '{target.keyword_label}', one that archives emails from {target.archive_domain} and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from {target.forward_sender} to {target.forward_to}. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.
gmail_filter_overhaul.instruction: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match billing-services.test but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label 'Billing'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security', one that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from elena.patel@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.

gmail_budget_reconciliation.title: Budget Reconciliation
gmail_budget_reconciliation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_budget_reconciliation.instruction_template: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. {target.dept_a_name} sent the {target.dept_a_dept} figures, {target.dept_b_name} sent the {target.dept_b_dept} figures, and {target.dept_c_name} sent the {target.dept_c_dept} figures. A fourth email from {target.summary_author_name} attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to {target.summary_author_name}'s email with the two corrections. Reply only to {target.summary_author_name} (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.
gmail_budget_reconciliation.instruction: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. Elena Patel sent the Engineering figures, Priya Rivera sent the Marketing figures, and Theo Brooks sent the Operations figures. A fourth email from Avery Kim attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to Avery Kim's email with the two corrections. Reply only to Avery Kim (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

gmail_label_workflow_setup.title: Label Workflow Setup
gmail_label_workflow_setup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_label_workflow_setup.instruction_template: Set up a label workflow for the new project. Create three labels: 'Project Alpha', 'Needs Review', and 'Client Feedback'. Then search for emails from {target.client_domain} and apply the 'Client Feedback' label to each. Search for emails with 'review' in the subject and apply the 'Needs Review' label to each. Apply the 'Project Alpha' label to all project-related emails — the ones from {target.client_domain} and the review emails. Then go to Settings > Labels and set 'Project Alpha' to show if unread, and set 'Client Feedback' to hidden. Finally, remove the 'Needs Review' label from the email about '{target.wrong_review_subject}' — it was mislabeled.

gmail_phishing_investigation.title: Phishing Investigation
gmail_phishing_investigation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_phishing_investigation.instruction_template: The security team flagged suspicious activity. Search for unread emails with attachments (use 'has:attachment is:unread') and review the results. You should find four emails — two are legitimate and two are phishing attempts. The phishing emails have a sender address that does not match the displayed name (check the actual email address). Delete the two phishing emails. Star the two legitimate emails. Compose a new email to {target.security_team_email} reporting your findings — include the subjects of both phishing emails in your report. Do NOT forward the phishing emails directly. Create a 'Verified Safe' label and apply it to the two legitimate emails. Then create a filter to auto-archive and mark-read future emails from the phishing domain.

gmail_new_hire_setup.title: New Hire Setup
gmail_new_hire_setup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_new_hire_setup.instruction_template: It is your first day. Set up your Gmail. Go to Settings and change: display density to comfortable, undo send to 30 seconds, default reply behavior to reply, and enable Send and Archive. Then create three team labels: 'Engineering', 'Design', and 'Product'. Set up two filters in Settings: one that stars emails from {target.team_list_email} and labels them 'Engineering', and one that stars emails from {target.manager_email}. Find the three welcome emails from your team leads in your inbox and add each as a contact (use their name, email, and company from the email). Reply to the welcome email from {target.welcome_sender_name} with the phrase '{target.intro_phrase}' and CC {target.hiring_manager_email} — but do not Reply All (the original email CC'd the entire team).

gmail_quarterly_closeout.title: Quarterly Closeout
gmail_quarterly_closeout.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_quarterly_closeout.instruction_template: End-of-quarter cleanup. In your Primary tab: star the two most important emails (the ones mentioning '{target.star_keyword_a}' and '{target.star_keyword_b}'), and archive the three old FYI emails from {target.fyi_sender_name}. In your Promotions tab: archive the three newsletters and delete the spam email about '{target.spam_subject}'. In your Updates tab: forward the two update emails about '{target.update_topic}' to {target.team_digest_email}. Create two labels: 'Q1 Active' and 'Q1 Archive'. Apply 'Q1 Active' to the two starred emails. Go to Contacts, delete the two contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Update the note for {target.update_contact_name} to '{target.new_note}'. Go to Settings > Filters and create a filter that archives emails from {target.vendor_domain} and labels them '{target.vendor_label}'. Change the maximum page size to 25.
